title: phyllota squarrosa
intro: Phyllota squarrosa, also known as New Zealand knapweed or tree knapweed, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its pinkish-purple flowers and spiny foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, as it is believed to possess antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Phyllota squarrosa include its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), reduce inflammation, and support the health of the digestive system. Phyllota squarrosa can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
